title: Uncaught storage errors can crash the client component
reasoning: All localStorage calls assume storage is available. In browsers or embedded contexts where storage access is blocked, full, or denied, getItem/setItem/removeItem can throw a DOMException. Because these calls are not guarded, the effect, tour teardown callback, or footer-triggered reset can throw into the client runtime instead of simply skipping persistence.
reproduction: Run the app in a browser context where localStorage is disabled or denied, then mount OnboardingTour or trigger resetAndStartTour. The unhandled localStorage exception interrupts the component behavior.
recommendation: Wrap localStorage access in a small safe helper that catches DOMException and treats storage as unavailable. The tour can still run without persistence, and reset can still fall back to reload or no-op behavior.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No tests are included for this feature, and no browser-storage failure path is exercised.
suggestedRegressionTest: Mock localStorage.getItem/setItem/removeItem to throw and assert that rendering OnboardingTour and calling resetAndStartTour do not throw.
minimumFixScope: Add guarded localStorage helpers in frontend/src/components/onboarding-tour.tsx and use them for get, set, and remove operations.
